meaning: adj. 反常的，异常的；变态的
example: The test results were abnormal.
The government decided to abolish the old law.

meaning: adj. 突然的；唐突的；陡峭的
example: His abrupt departure surprised everyone.
example_trans: 他突然离开让所有人都吃惊。

example: That's an absurd thing to say!
The car accelerated smoothly.
She bought some accessories for her phone.

meaning: v. 容纳；提供住宿；适应
example: The hotel can accommodate up to 500 guests.
example_trans: 这家酒店最多可容纳500位客人。

example: Dust had accumulated on the shelves.

meaning: v. 使熟悉，使了解；通知
example: I want to acquaint you with the facts.
The company made several acquisitions this year.
example_trans: 公司今年进行了几次收购。

example: Press the button to activate the alarm.

meaning: adj. 敏锐的；急性的；严重的
example: She has an acute sense of smell.
The paint adheres well to the surface.
example_trans: 油漆很好地附着在表面上。

example: Our offices are adjacent to each other.
example_trans: 我们的办公室彼此相邻。

example: It takes skill to administer a large company.

meaning: adj. 青春期的 n. 青少年
example: Adolescents need plenty of sleep.
example_trans: 青少年需要充足的睡眠。

example: The advent of the internet changed everything.
example_trans: 互联网的出现改变了一切。

meaning: adj. 不利的；相反的；有害的
example: The drug may have adverse effects.

meaning: v. 提倡，拥护 n. 倡导者
example: He advocates for better public transport.

meaning: adj. 美学的；审美的；艺术的
example: The room has great aesthetic appeal.
example_trans: 这个房间有很强的美感。

meaning: v. 使隶属；加入 n. 分支机构
example: The company is affiliated with a larger group.
example_trans: 该公司隶属于一个更大的集团。

example: She affirmed her commitment to the project.
example_trans: 她确认了对项目的承诺。

example: She is afflicted with arthritis.

meaning: n. 议程，日程；待办事项
example: Poverty reduction is high on the agenda.
Don't scratch - it will aggravate the wound.

meaning: n. 总数，合计 adj. 总计的
example: The aggregate cost was over $1 million.
He denied the allegations against him.
Soldiers pledge allegiance to their country.
The medicine helps alleviate pain.
example_trans: 这种药有助于缓解疼痛。

example: Funds were allocated for research.

meaning: adj. 模棱两可的；含糊的
example: His answer was ambiguous.

meaning: adj. 有雄心的；野心勃勃的
example: She has ambitious plans for her career.
example_trans: 她对自己的职业有宏伟的计划。

example: The law was amended last year.
example_trans: 这项法律去年被修订了。

meaning: adj. 充足的，丰富的；宽敞的
example: There's ample time to finish the task.
example_trans: 有充足的时间完成任务。

example: He drew an analogy between life and a journey.
example_trans: 他把人生比作一次旅行。

example: The donor wished to remain anonymous.
We anticipate good results from the project.
example_trans: 我们期待项目能产生好结果。

example: It became apparent that she was right.
example_trans: 她是对的，这一点变得很明显。

example: They tried to appease the angry crowd.
example_trans: 他们试图安抚愤怒的人群。

example: The audience applauded loudly.
All employees get an annual appraisal.
example_trans: 所有员工都有年度评估。

example: The police apprehended the suspect.

meaning: v. 接近；处理 n. 方法
example: We need a new approach to this problem.
example_trans: 我们需要一个解决这个问题的新方法。

example: Please wear appropriate clothing.
The committee approved the proposal.

meaning: adj. 恰当的；易于...的；聪明的
example: He is apt to forget things.
The decision seemed completely arbitrary.
example_trans: 这个决定似乎完全是任意的。

example: Problems may arise in the future.
His strange behavior aroused suspicion.
example_trans: 他奇怪的行为引起了怀疑。

meaning: v. 明确表达 adj. 善于表达的
example: She is very articulate for her age.
example_trans: 就她的年龄而言，她很善于表达。
